examples.text: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَاكُم مِن بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
examples.english: But We raised you again after you had been as dead, so that you might have cause to be grateful.

senses.gloss: a red gemstone, cornelian, garnet, spinel, tourmaline

etymology.text:
Univerbation of لَـ (la-, “truly, indeed, verily”) and عَلَّ (ʕalla).

From Persian لال (lâl).

body.text: ]; a word denoting hope and fear. (K.) It governs the subject in the accus. case, and the predicate in the nom. See Ibn-'Akeel, ed. of Dieterici, p. 90; and see
, in its original and general acceptation, expresses hope; but in the word of God it [often] expresses certainty, and may be rendered
